key_insight: The paper gives exact worst- and best-case distorted utility values under moment ambiguity by solving the inner distributional-robust problem in quantile space with nonsmooth optimality conditions and isotonic projection.
engineering_relevance: This provides a drop-in oracle for robust optimization in pricing, inventory, capacity, and contract design, helping engineers hedge against ambiguous demand while keeping computations scalable.
method: Reformulate the ambiguous utility problem in the quantile domain.
Derive exact first-order conditions and closed-form extremal values/distributions for both worst and best cases.
Handle monotonicity via isotonic projection onto the monotone cone for a linear-time inner solve after discretization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation through three benchmark examples plus a real capacity-provisioning case for generative AI inference, where moment ambiguity changes the capacity needed to meet service targets.
watch_item: The method still relies on moment information and careful numerical treatment of nonsmooth utilities and distortion functions, so implementation details and discretization quality matter.
paper_title: Extreme-Case Distorted Utility under Moment Ambiguity
